feed.title: Yudhanjaya Wijeratne — Posts
feed.subtitle: The homeppage of author Yudhanjaya Wijeratne. Links, information, updates, and other writings.

entry.title: Can You Vibe-Code a Novel?
entry.summary: A 33,000-word experiment: using Claude 3.7 Sonnet and Gemini to generate a novel premise, setting, characters, and plot outline — and asking whether AI's real limitation is capability or artistic motivation.

entry.title: The Fate of the Gorillas
entry.summary: A framework for thinking about AI: faster intelligence vs. stranger intelligence, and what superintelligence might actually look like when you look past the chatbots.

entry.title: My Low Power Writing Setup
entry.summary: A minimalist writing station running at 24 watts: an Android gaming handheld, a wireless keyboard, and Obsidian. Built for distraction-free writing during Sri Lanka's power cuts.

entry.title: The Ricepunk Manifesto
entry.summary: Towards a new theme, or a horrendously jumbled set of concerns. A framework for science fiction that centers South Asian relationships, hybrid cultures, and the shit sandwich.

entry.title: Mammals and Dinosaurs
entry.summary: The Wild West of indie publishing is settling down. A look at collaborative publishers, franchise IP, and what the future of the book industry might look like.

entry.title: The Steam Hammer
entry.summary: A speech given at the Arthur C Clarke Centennial, December 2017: on AI, John Henry, and whether you die with the steam hammer or learn to use it.
